question_text: Which numerical summary is resistant to extreme outliers?
choices: [{"id": "a", "text": "Interquartile range"}, {"id": "b", "text": "Mean"}, {"id": "c", "text": "Standard deviation"}, {"id": "d", "text": "Median"}]
hint: Focus on the phrase in the question that names the statistical idea, not on the answer choices first.
explanation: Extreme values affect some summaries more than others. The mean and standard deviation use the actual sizes of all observations, so outliers and skewness can pull them. The median, quartiles, and IQR are more resistant because they depend more on order or position. In this item, **Median** is the best choice because it matches what the question is asking you to identify.

question_text: An online poll allows anyone who sees the link to respond. What does this kind of biased method tend to produce?

explanation: Sampling questions focus on how the data were produced.
